entries.summary: Group attained-service ordering is recognizable, but PLAS queue bands, quanta, demotion, starvation reset, and ATLAS critical-path state are absent.
high_confidence_fixes: Normalize waiting and service units.
Implement PLAS queues, quanta, demotion and starvation promotion.
Add an explicit ATLAS critical-path mode.
benchmark_corrections: Use sequential and fork/join program traces.
Measure program JCT, throughput and p95/p99 starvation versus FCFS and MLFQ.

entries.summary: The preempted, pinned, arrival ordering is present, but optimized TTL is reduced to a Boolean and expiry lacks time, history, pressure and same-program queue semantics.
high_confidence_fixes: Store expiration timestamps, tool histories and pressure.
Implement the empirical-CDF TTL utility search.
Reclaim only enough unpinned objects.
benchmark_corrections: Replay multi-turn tool-duration traces under memory pressure.
Compare LRU, fixed-2s, optimized and oracle TTL.

entries.summary: The eviction tuple and prefetch intent resemble KVFlow, but Agent Step Graph aggregation and transfer states are absent.
high_confidence_fixes: Hard-filter non-ready/loading work and honor max selections.
Represent shared-node STE plus GPU, CPU, loading and offloading states.
Reclaim only the required byte prefix.
benchmark_corrections: Exercise AND/OR DAGs with shared radix nodes and transfer overlap.
Measure workflow makespan and cache-stall time.

entries.summary: The exploit/explore threshold is present, but exploration omits per-target profiled cost and exploitation omits least-load tie-breaking and post-placement behavior.
high_confidence_fixes: Compute per-target L_i plus M_i plus P_i.
Break equal prefix hits by load.
Model future-request redirection.
benchmark_corrections: Use the five paper workloads and Azure arrivals.
Measure RPS and mean/P99 E2E against distributed SGLang and vLLM.

entries.summary: One local ready/depth/reuse choice does not reproduce Helium's whole-DAG TRT planner, multi-worker assignment, nested sequences or capacity timeline.
high_confidence_fixes: Run a whole-DAG reference planner.
Represent TRT sharing, profiled outputs, precedence delays and worker capacities.
benchmark_corrections: Execute complete batched DAGs on multiple workers.
Compare query-wise, op-wise, random and LSPF using makespan and token steps.

entries.summary: Minimum-counter ordering is recognizable, but the original implementation lacked counter lift, weighted costs, dispatch charging and iterative batch accounting.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement Algorithm 2 counter lift and per-client FIFO state.
Charge weighted input at dispatch and output deltas during decode.
Filter feedback by request/progress semantics.
benchmark_corrections: Replay ON/OFF, heterogeneous-length, Poisson and distribution-shift traces.
Measure service disparity versus the theorem bound, TTFT, throughput and utilization.

entries.summary: The P-token times batch-size core exists, but idle-replica semantics and the stateful two-phase hotspot detector are absent.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement artifact-equivalent batch-size plus one behavior.
Add popularity/cache-coverage history, confirmation and load-only fallback.
benchmark_corrections: Use Qwen ChatBot, Qwen Agent, Bailian Coder and Kimi ToolAgent.
Test idle replicas and multi-decision hotspot sequences.

entries.summary: The original kernel confused interaction-aware throttling with an invented interference score and omitted OIT and application-stage WSC normalization.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement overload-gated user/application RPM throttling.
Preserve in-progress interactions.
Implement equations 2-3 with application-stage token accounting.
benchmark_corrections: Use abusive users, overload intervals and multi-call applications.
Measure wasted tokens, queue delay, TTFT and prompt/decode throughput versus VTC and RPM.

entries.summary: A reuse-times-FLOPs-per-byte knapsack replaces Marconi's separate speculative admission and recency-plus-FLOP-efficiency eviction.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement speculative radix-tree admission.
Use normalized recency plus alpha times FLOP efficiency.
Support one-child eligibility, SSM-only reclaim and KV merging.
benchmark_corrections: Use hybrid-model LMSys, ShareGPT and SWE-bench traces.
Compare timestamped LRU and artifact decision traces.

entries.summary: A static lowest-score leaf resembles PGDSF, but clocks, updates, iterative frontier evolution and GPU-host hierarchy are missing.
high_confidence_fixes: Maintain GPU/host clocks, frequency and profiled average cost.
Iterate leaf eviction and expose newly eligible parents.
Represent GPU, host and free tiers.
benchmark_corrections: Use timestamped LRU and parent-child episodes.
Compare vLLM+Faiss and SGLang using TTFT and throughput.

entries.summary: Deficit and locality signals are present, but the original implementation reversed locality-first ordering and omitted refill cycles, extend accounting and per-worker deficits.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement LPM-first positive-deficit scanning and quantum refill.
Charge uncached extend and decode output tokens.
Maintain per-worker deficits and longest-prefix worker sets.
benchmark_corrections: Use stateful multi-client radix-tree traces.
Measure hit rate, recomputation, Jain fairness, throughput and victim latency versus VTC, LPM and Preble.

entries.summary: The kernel conflates interception swap priority with resume scheduling and substitutes a non-paper reuse-versus-recompute predicate for preserve/swap/discard waste.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement preserve and chunk-discard waste equations.
Allocate swap budget in descending minimum-waste order.
Separate FCFS resume queues and explicit preserve/swap/discard states.
benchmark_corrections: Run the mixed augmentation workload against vLLM, discard, preserve and swap.
Measure normalized latency, throughput, TTFT and memory waste.

entries.summary: Only the demand-and-fairness idea remains; pending trie, cLPM sections, stride lanes, dynamic fairness and ancestor-demand eviction are absent.
high_confidence_fixes: Maintain pending-prefix state and exact cLPM facts.
Implement warm/pioneer/sibling ordering and stride lane interleaving.
Return only the capacity-required victim set.
benchmark_corrections: Use SGLang LPM+LRU and vLLM FCFS+APC+LRU.
Run W1-W5 and report cache hit, TTFT, E2E and throughput.

entries.summary: Independent SLO gates and scalar wait ordering do not implement request grouping, RWT estimation, virtual queues or the global LP.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement grouped virtual queues and equations 1-5 RWT estimation.
Implement equations 6-13 global assignment and ordering.
Model pulling, eviction, swapping and load balancing.
benchmark_corrections: Run event-driven multi-model virtual queues.
Measure actual TTFT-SLO attainment, throughput, utilization, swaps and evictions versus EDF, FCFS and SHEPHERD.

entries.summary: Independent feasibility gates and least-slack order omit the defining joint DP, performance model, mixed token batches, speculation and best-effort fallback.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement joint value-maximizing admission under memory and stage SLOs.
Generate mixed prefill/decode batches from a profiled performance model.
Add speculation and best-effort or replica fallback.
benchmark_corrections: Replay multi-stage burst workloads.
Measure requests/GPU/s below 10% stage-SLO violations versus vLLM, Sarathi and DistServe.

entries.summary: Thresholded termination remains, but Certaindex computation, stable-answer preservation, program-level knobs, gang scheduling and SJF are absent.
high_confidence_fixes: Compute Certaindex from answer, entropy or reward histories.
Return the stable answer instead of cancelling it.
Represent reasoning programs and gang/SJF scheduling.
benchmark_corrections: Replay real intermediate answers and uncertainty.
Measure tokens-to-accuracy, equal-quality savings, throughput and probe overhead.

entries.summary: Fewest-completed-branches is not Justitia's predicted KV token-time cost and GPS virtual-finish scheduling.
high_confidence_fixes: Maintain GPS virtual time and immutable finish tags.
Select the minimum-tag agent and bundle all fitting ready requests.
benchmark_corrections: Use heterogeneous agent arrivals and finite KV capacity.
Measure average/P90 JCT and finish-time fairness versus VTC, Parrot, FCFS and SRJF.

entries.summary: Queue hints exist, but token quotas, two-phase redistribution, head-of-line bypass and the cost-aware adapter cache are absent.
high_confidence_fixes: Track per-queue token quotas and spare redistribution.
Implement real head-of-line adapter bypass.
Use dynamic cache sizing, reference counts and the paper cache score.
benchmark_corrections: Use S-LoRA and report P99/P50 TTFT plus SLO throughput.
Exercise quota phases, bypass and cache pressure.

entries.summary: A cumulative-count threshold at primary admission does not implement clocked GPU leaf eviction, host admission or host-to-GPU promotion.
high_confidence_fixes: Track frequency, clock, length, tier and parent-child state.
Implement GPU leaf priority and host hotness comparison.
Implement constrained post-prefill promotion.
benchmark_corrections: Use MMLU, Hellaswag, BBH and CEVAL.
Compare LRU, LFU, FIFO and 2Q using hit rate, latency, throughput and offload time.

entries.summary: The original two-term P95 gate omitted PARD's queue, execution, batch-wait quantile, pipeline state and adaptive HBF/LBF ordering.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement equation 3 with queue, execution and F^-1(0.1) terms.
Maintain sliding windows and DAG-path latency.
Add load-driven HBF/LBF with hysteresis.
benchmark_corrections: Replay bursty multi-module and DAG pipelines.
Measure SLO goodput, drops, invalid GPU time and drop location versus Nexus, Clipper and naive.

entries.summary: A static group cap does not implement TAPER's protected token, slack-budgeted widening and marginal utility planner; cancellation contradicts reversible deferral.
high_confidence_fixes: Grant protected progress each decode step.
Compute T(S), slack and rho-scaled budget.
Defer excess branches without cancelling the request.
benchmark_corrections: Replay serial/parallel phases under low, moderate and high load.
Compare IRP-Off, C2, C5 and Eager using SLO token goodput and P99 TPOT.

entries.summary: Cache-hit and risk vocabulary is present, but two-choice hashing, SLO switching and benefit-ranked queued rebalancing are absent.
high_confidence_fixes: Generate exactly two adaptive-prefix hash candidates.
Implement the TTFT-SLO switch.
Rebalance queued requests to alternate candidates by benefit.
benchmark_corrections: Use Cache Affinity, Least Loaded, Min TTFT and Preble.
Measure effective request capacity, goodput, TTFT/E2E, hit rate and load CV.

entries.summary: One virtual-usage scalar omits Llumnix freeness, usage rules, pairing, victim selection and migration coordination.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement HOL demand, priority headroom and physical usage.
Dispatch by freeness and pair source/destination instances.
Model migration handshake and rollback.
benchmark_corrections: Use evolving ShareGPT/BurstGPT queues versus round-robin, INFaaS and Llumnix-base.
Measure mean/P99 E2E, prefill, decode, fragmentation and downtime.

entries.summary: The first/follow-up split exists, but overload and eviction guards are absent and active migration differs from global-tier fallback routing.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement overload and likely-eviction guards.
Fallback to load balancing when either guard fails.
Represent global-tier fetch instead of active migration.
benchmark_corrections: Replay multi-request sessions with local/global KV capacity.
Measure TPS under TTFT/TPOT SLO, hit location and load imbalance.

entries.summary: Tool-ready filtering and caller-supplied migration do not implement reasoning/acting lifecycle, thrashing detection, pause/restore or tool-resource management.
high_confidence_fixes: Add reasoning/acting and active/paused/terminated states.
Implement periodic victim selection and capacity-aware restore.
Require enacted migration feedback.
benchmark_corrections: Use multi-backend programs with context growth and tool intervals.
Measure throughput, recomputation, hit rate, imbalance and tool resources.

entries.summary: Generic lookahead, rank and next-use scalars do not implement lineage-aware caching, OOM-safe headroom routing or dynamic graph priority.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement future lineage keep/drop and exact prompt staging.
Implement union-bound OOM admission and headroom routing.
Implement expected-distance, downstream-idle and aging priority.
benchmark_corrections: Use workflow regexes, output distributions and constrained replicas.
Measure average/P95 JCT, throughput, TTFT and OOM rate.

entries.summary: Quality-gap/cost ordering is not GoodServe's E2E-SLO goodput objective or just-enough GPU selection.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement q+p(Lin-H)+dLout latency prediction.
Choose the least capable GPU meeting the SLO.
Add periodic stronger just-enough rerouting.
benchmark_corrections: Use BIRD, SWE-bench and LiveCodeBench with Mooncake arrivals.
Measure E2E-SLO goodput and violation ratio versus random, load, Preble and Llumnix.

entries.summary: Only first-turn versus follow-up routing remains; prefiller role, one-time KV transfer, lowest-KV decoder binding and capacity equations are absent.
high_confidence_fixes: Route first turns to a prefiller and bind follow-ups to the lowest-KV decoder.
Persist decoder binding across generations.
Implement exactly-once transfer and capacity provisioning.
benchmark_corrections: Use persistent SWE-bench conversations.
Measure TTFET, last-turn TBT, E2E, SLO violations and energy versus collocated, disaggregated and AMPD.

entries.summary: Dependency readiness is only a prerequisite; criteria propagation, task groups, prefix/context grouping, capacity checks and interference-aware placement are absent.
high_confidence_fixes: Produce trusted DAG depth, criteria, task-group and prefix facts.
Schedule groups atomically and place them capacity-aware with context affinity.
benchmark_corrections: Run chain, map-reduce and multi-agent Semantic Variable DAGs.
Measure makespan and sustainable throughput against FCFS and least queue.

entries.summary: Always-locality routing, generic group service and Boolean TTL omit WA-LRU, adaptive TTL, guarded stealing, KV migration and deadline-based AFS.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement AEG-derived reuse, WA-LRU and pressure-scaled TTL.
Implement the affinity threshold and guarded steal victim selection.
Implement AFS deadline feedback and affinity updates.
benchmark_corrections: Use multi-step sessions with tool durations, pressure and deadlines.
Measure TCT, memory utilization, regeneration and SLO attainment versus vLLM+APC.

entries.summary: Exhaustive static utility matching is the opposite of RouteBalance's normalized quality/latency/cost score and LPT greedy dead-reckoning.
high_confidence_fixes: Implement normalized three-weight scoring and cost budgets.
Sort by predicted output length and update instance state after each assignment.
Remove the exponential 12-request bound.
benchmark_corrections: Use the seven named datasets and official artifact decision traces.
Measure quality, completed requests/s, E2E latency and realized cost.
